mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that historically precede earnings downgrades, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses the 6.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving revenue growth across cyclical sectors and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS in a multivariate regression shows a coefficient with p-value greater than 0.10 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production expands supply-side capacity, which suppresses input cost inflation and supports corporate margin expansion, particularly when headline inflation is already contained, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises for three consecutive months but SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations due to supply chain bottlenecks overriding capacity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation exceeding a 0.4 percent month-over-month rate forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX rallies despite CPI acceleration exceeding the threshold because markets successfully price in a rapid disinflation trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2.0 percent annualized trend line reflects underlying economic momentum that translates directly into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP beats the 2.0 percent trend but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicates a soft landing environment where labor demand supports earnings without triggering restrictive monetary policy, maximizing equity upside over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS move in the predicted joint direction but SPX forward returns show no statistical premium (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained collapse in housing starts falling below 750 thousand units for two consecutive months acts as an early warning signal for broader economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 750 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: HOUST drops below the 750 threshold but SPX 5-day returns remain positive due to anticipated fiscal stimulus or rate cut expectations overriding the housing signal.
